data.title: BSB listed on Bitget PoolX, unlocking 1.13 million BSB from locked BTC.
data.description: Bitget PoolX is launching the BSB project, with a total airdrop of 1,133,000 BSB. There are two BTC staking pools. In the regular pool, users can lock up to 30 BTC to unlock 400,000 BSB. In the dynamic cap pool, the unlock limit is tiered based on the user's trading volume over the past 15 days, with a maximum lock-up of 35 BTC per user, unlocking 733,000 BSB. The staking channel is open from 16:00 on March 5th to 16:00 on March 10th (UTC+8). Users with a positive net BTC deposit during the event will receive a 5% BTC interest rate bonus coupon after PoolX ends. Additionally, first-time PoolX participants who meet the net deposit requirements will receive a 10% BTC interest rate bonus coupon. Net top-up period ends on March 9th at 12:00 (UTC+8). For more details, please visit the Bitget official platform.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister condemns US attack on Iranian warship
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi said today (March 5) that the United States, by launching a surprise attack on an Iranian warship in international waters, "will ultimately regret the precedent it set." Araqchi stated on social media that the Iranian frigate, carrying approximately 130 people, was a "guest of the Indian Navy" and was attacked in international waters 2,000 miles (approximately 3,218 kilometers) off the Iranian coast without any prior warning. US Defense Secretary Hergsays said on March 4 that a US submarine torpedoed and sank an Iranian warship "believing itself to be safe in international waters" in the Indian Ocean on March 3. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iraqi Kurdistan Pledges Peace Amidst Regional Tensions
data.description:
On March 5, Nechirvan Barzani, the President of the Kurdistan Region in northern Iraq, issued a statement emphasizing the region's commitment to peace and its refusal to engage in conflicts or military actions that endanger civilian lives. According to Jin10, Barzani called for unity among all political forces, parties, and communities within the Kurdistan Region to collectively uphold national responsibilities.

Aziz Ahmed, Deputy Chief of Staff of the Kurdistan Regional Government, also addressed the situation on social media platform X, refuting claims that Iraqi Kurds had crossed borders, labeling such reports as "clearly false." This statement comes in response to earlier reports from the United States suggesting that Iraqi Kurds had launched a ground offensive within Iran.

data.title: Sungrow Partners with Egyptian Contractor for Major Solar Project
data.description: Sungrow has announced a significant collaboration with Egyptian project contractor Hassan Allam to supply centralized photovoltaic inverter solutions for the Benban Solar Park in Aswan, Egypt. According to Jin10, this agreement marks another major solar project for Sungrow in North Africa, following their supply to the Abydos energy storage station in Egypt.

data.title: ECB Vice President De Guindos: Iranian Attack Heightens Uncertainty
data.description: European Central Bank Vice President Luis de Guindos has expressed concerns over the increased uncertainty following recent attacks by Iran. According to Jin10, De Guindos highlighted the geopolitical tensions that could impact economic stability and market conditions. The situation has raised questions about potential repercussions for the global economy, particularly in terms of energy prices and investor sentiment. De Guindos emphasized the need for vigilance and careful monitoring of developments as the situation unfolds.

data.title: Iran Open to Abandoning Nuclear Program if U.S. Offers Satisfactory Alternative
data.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister has announced that Iran is willing to abandon its nuclear program, provided that the United States proposes a satisfactory alternative. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid ongoing tensions between the two nations over nuclear capabilities and international security concerns. The Iranian official emphasized the need for a viable and acceptable solution from the U.S. to address the nuclear issue. This development could potentially pave the way for renewed diplomatic negotiations and efforts to resolve the longstanding conflict over Iran's nuclear ambitions.

data.title: South Korea Bans Travel to Iran Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: South Korea has announced a travel ban to Iran starting Thursday at 6 PM, according to Jin10. This decision by the South Korean Ministry of Foreign Affairs comes amid escalating tensions in the region. The move aims to ensure the safety of South Korean citizens in light of the current geopolitical climate. Travelers are advised to adhere to the new regulations and avoid non-essential travel to the area. The ministry will continue to monitor the situation closely and provide updates as necessary.

data.title: Majority of AI Models Choose Bitcoin as Top Monetary Preference: Report
data.description:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) platforms prefer Bitcoin (BTC) over other crypto and fiat payment methods, according to a new study.

data.title: Momenta Files for IPO in Hong Kong
data.description: Momenta has submitted a confidential application for an initial public offering (IPO) in Hong Kong. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing that the autonomous driving technology company is seeking to raise funds through this IPO. The move comes as Momenta aims to expand its operations and enhance its technological capabilities in the competitive autonomous driving sector. The company, known for its advancements in self-driving technology, is positioning itself to capitalize on the growing demand for autonomous vehicles. The IPO filing marks a significant step in Momenta's growth strategy, as it seeks to leverage the financial markets to support its development initiatives. Details regarding the size and timing of the IPO have not been disclosed, but the filing indicates Momenta's intention to strengthen its market presence and drive innovation in the industry.

data.title: Russia Vows to Support Iran Against Potential Actions
data.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov stated that Russia and its partners will make every effort to create an environment where any actions against Iran become impossible. According to Jin10, Lavrov emphasized the importance of collaboration in ensuring stability and preventing any aggressive moves towards Iran. This statement reflects Russia's commitment to supporting its allies and maintaining regional peace.

data.title: Dogecoin Faces Challenges Amid Declining Investor Confidence
data.description: Dogecoin, which reached a peak of $0.73 in May 2021 following support from Elon Musk, has experienced a significant decline and is currently trading at approximately $0.09. According to NS3.AI, for Dogecoin to rise to 50 cents, it would require a 450% increase and a market capitalization of $140 billion. However, this scenario seems unlikely due to waning retail enthusiasm and limited institutional interest. Investor confidence has diminished after enduring sustained losses, making a substantial price surge for Dogecoin improbable in the near future.

data.title: Brother Machi added another 100 ETH to his long position, with a floating profit of $245,000.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Brother Machi has added another 100 ETH to his long position, bringing his current position value to $14.56 million and his unrealized profit to $245,000.

data.title: ING's Chris Turner Predicts Limited GBP Strength Against Euro
data.description: Chris Turner from ING has highlighted in a report that the recent strengthening of the British pound against the euro is largely due to position adjustments. According to Jin10, Turner suggests that this upward trend is unlikely to be sustained. Asset management firms currently hold significant net short positions on the pound, betting on its decline, while maintaining long positions on the euro, anticipating its rise. The market has revised its expectations for a rate cut by the Bank of England, providing further support for the pound. Although ING now expects the Bank of England to cut rates in April instead of March, it still forecasts two rate cuts this year. Turner believes this will lead the euro to rise above 0.88 pounds.

data.title: South Korea Implements $68.2 Billion Market Stabilization Program Amid Volatility
data.description: South Korea's President Lee Jae Myung has announced a $68.2 billion market stabilization program to address the country's unprecedented market volatility. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's efforts to stabilize the financial markets during this turbulent period. The initiative aims to bolster investor confidence and mitigate the impact of economic fluctuations. The program includes measures to support liquidity and ensure the smooth functioning of financial systems. President Lee emphasized the importance of maintaining stability in the face of global economic challenges. The government is committed to implementing strategies that will safeguard the nation's economic interests and promote sustainable growth.

data.title: Germany's February Construction PMI Declines
data.description: Germany's construction sector experienced a decline in February, with the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) falling to 43.7 from the previous value of 44.7. According to Jin10, this decrease indicates a contraction in the construction industry, as a PMI below 50 suggests a reduction in activity. The drop in the index reflects ongoing challenges within the sector, potentially influenced by broader economic conditions. The construction industry continues to face headwinds, impacting its overall performance.

data.title: European Natural Gas Prices Surge Amid Qatar LNG Supply Disruption
data.description: European natural gas prices experienced a significant increase following Qatar's announcement of a production halt at a key liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility. According to Jin10, Qatar, the world's largest LNG exporter, declared force majeure on shipments, causing a ripple effect across the global gas market. Analysts from ANZ Bank highlighted the sensitivity of the timing, noting that inventories are currently low, particularly in Europe, as the heating season in Asia and Europe nears its end. With limited alternative sources, replenishing stocks in the coming months may pose challenges for consumers. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract price rose by 10.5% to €53.87 per megawatt-hour, marking a 90% increase since the beginning of the year.

data.title: STOCKS | IFBH Denies Sugar Additive Allegations, Shares Surge
data.description: IFBH (6603), a coconut water company, recently faced allegations regarding the addition of external sugars to its products. According to Ming Pao, the company issued a statement denying these claims. Following the announcement, IFBH's stock price saw a significant increase, rising as much as 50.33% to a peak of 15.77 yuan. The latest report shows the stock at 11.62 yuan, marking a 10.77% increase.

data.title: Nick Szabo Warns Against Storing Data on Bitcoin Blockchain
data.description:
Bitcoin core developer Nick Szabo has expressed concerns about the use of the Bitcoin blockchain for storing images or files through a method known as Inscriptions. According to NS3.AI, Szabo emphasized that Bitcoin was originally designed as a financial protocol rather than a general data archive. He cautioned that embedding illegal content could result in regulatory challenges.

Inscriptions allow data to be embedded onto satoshis, thereby broadening Bitcoin's applications. However, this development has sparked worries about the immutability of the blockchain and the possibility of government crackdowns. Szabo's warning highlights the potential risks associated with expanding Bitcoin's use cases beyond its intended financial scope.
